title: Position PnL ignores filled execution prices

evidence.quote: Fill writes FilledPrice and FilledAmount but leaves Price unchanged.
CostBasis and ExitValue multiply Amount by Price.
Tests create a NewOrderDetail market order, call Fill, then pass it to record.Operate.

reasoning: NewOrderDetail creates market orders with Price left at zero, while Fill records the actual execution in FilledPrice. Any Position created from a normally filled market order therefore has zero cost basis and zero exit value, corrupting PnL, drawdown, exposure, and stop calculations.
reproduction: order := NewOrderDetail(BUY, MarketOrder, "TEST", decimal.New(10)); order.Fill(decimal.New(100), decimal.New(10)); position := NewPosition(*order); position.CostBasis() returns 0 instead of 1000.
recommendation: Make Position valuation use FilledPrice/FilledAmount when an order is filled, or make Fill synchronize the execution price/amount fields used by Position. Preserve limit price semantics separately if Price is intended to remain the submitted limit.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: orders_test.go asserts FilledPrice/FilledAmount after Fill, while position_test.go builds positions from literal Orders with Price populated. No test combines Fill with Position.CostBasis or ExitValue.
suggestedRegressionTest: Create filled market entry and exit orders via NewOrderDetail + Fill, operate them through TradingRecord, and assert CostBasis, ExitValue, and realized PnL use the filled prices.
minimumFixScope: pkg/trading/order.go and/or pkg/trading/position.go valuation semantics, with TradingRecord/Position regression coverage.
